allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cold serving of bamboo salt white coffee is approximately 300 ml (300 g), served in a cup or takeaway cup with ice.

nutrient_source.title: White Coffee, prepared with non-dairy creamer and sugar — estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for coffee with creamer and sugar, adapted for bamboo salt white coffee formulation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for bamboo salt white coffee exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for coffee beverage with non-dairy creamer and sugar (NDB 14209 and related entries), adjusted for a typical Malaysian/Singaporean white coffee formulation (robusta coffee, non-dairy creamer, sugar, bamboo salt). Bamboo salt contributes a small amount of sodium. Per-100g values reflect a diluted ready-to-drink cold beverage at approximately 300 ml serving size. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee beverages with sugar and non-dairy creamer — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened coffee beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bamboo salt white coffee. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened white coffee and coffee with non-dairy creamer and sugar, which report GI values in the range 39–53. Bamboo salt does not materially affect glycemic response. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as (45 × 6.5 × 3) / 100 = 8.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium from bamboo salt and non-dairy creamer; caffeine may transiently raise blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake of this beverage due to its elevated sodium content (~165 mg per 300 ml serving from bamboo salt) and the potential transient blood pressure elevation from caffeine; occasional consumption is acceptable but daily high-volume intake is not recommended.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content contributing to glycemic load; GI approximately 45 (low) but serving-level sugar is significant
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this beverage as each 300 ml serving contains approximately 17 g of sugar, contributing a glycemic load of ~8.8; portion control and requesting reduced-sugar preparation are advisable to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from non-dairy creamer (palm oil base) and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of this beverage as non-dairy creamer contributes saturated fat (~2.7 g per 300 ml serving) and the added sugar load may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bamboo Salt White Coffee (Cold) is a specialty iced coffee beverage served at Hai Kah Lang restaurant in Singapore. It is a variation of the traditional Malaysian-style white coffee (白咖啡), which originated in Ipoh, Malaysia, and is characterised by robusta coffee beans roasted with palm oil margarine and blended with non-dairy creamer and sugar to produce a smooth, mildly sweet, and creamy coffee drink. The distinguishing feature of this version is the addition of bamboo salt (竹盐, zhu yan), a mineral-rich salt produced by packing sea salt into bamboo tubes and firing them at high temperatures, a process originating from Korean traditional medicine. Bamboo salt imparts a subtle mineral complexity and a slightly alkaline character to the coffee, differentiating it from standard white coffee. The cold version is served chilled or over ice, making it a refreshing beverage suited to Singapore's tropical climate. Nutritionally, per 100 ml the beverage provides approximately 38 kcal, 6.5 g carbohydrate (predominantly from added sugar), 1.2 g fat (mainly saturated fat from non-dairy creamer), and 0.5 g protein. Sodium content is elevated relative to plain coffee due to the bamboo salt addition, at approximately 55 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category), though the serving-level sugar load warrants moderation for individuals managing blood glucose. Clinically, this beverage is of moderate concern for hypertension (sodium and caffeine), Type 2 Diabetes (added sugar), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from non-dairy creamer). It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal.

Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for sweetened white coffee with non-dairy creamer, adapted for the bamboo salt variant. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for similar sweetened coffee beverages. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
